body: {"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. New test TestServerStartupReconcile_SkipsOrPrunesStaleDesiredProjects seeds stale desired-state/project entries and proves startup reconcile does not spawn repeat exited workers for missing or invalid projects.","coverage":"child 1 AC1 and AC3 cover stale desired-state/project seeding and prevention of repeat exited-worker churn for missing or invalid projects."},{"parent_ac":"2. TestServerStartupReconcile_SkipsOrPrunesStaleDesiredProjects proves an audit/error is exposed with the stale project path.","coverage":"child 2 AC1 and AC2 cover audit/error exposure and exact stale project path inclusion."},{"parent_ac":"3. TestServerStartupReconcile_SkipsOrPrunesStaleDesiredProjects proves valid desired worker state for active projects is preserved while stale entries are skipped or pruned.","coverage":"child 1 AC2 covers preservation during server reconcile; child 2 AC3 covers no false stale audit for valid projects; child 3 AC2 covers preservation in status JSON."},{"parent_ac":"4. `ddx worker status --json --project \u003cpath\u003e` after restart reports no unrelated stale-project workers for the seeded stale paths.","coverage":"child 3 AC1 and AC3 cover post-restart/status-query absence of unrelated stale-project workers for seeded stale paths."},{"parent_ac":"5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` passes.","coverage":"child 1 AC4 covers `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/...`; child 2 AC4 covers `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/...`; child 3 AC4 covers the full `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/server/... ./cmd/...` gate."},{"parent_ac":"6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","coverage":"child 1 AC5, child 2 AC5, and child 3 AC5 each require `lefthook run pre-commit`."}],"child_ids":["ddx-d4d764ea","ddx-20faafde","ddx-4868c1f7"],"rationale":"The parent bead spans three separable risks: server-side stale project filtering before worker spawn, operator-visible audit/error reporting, and CLI/status JSON behavior after restart. Splitting along those boundaries keeps each child independently executable with focused tests while preserving the parent non-scope, governing artifact reference, labels, and verification gates."}

summary: closed as dead-intermediate bead (all children closed, execution-eligible=false)
